debate_rounds.title: Chair Initial Assessment
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 9 adds scoring benchmarks and edge case tests. The _score_benchmark function validates the scoring model against known inputs (empty article scores low, keyword-dense article scores moderate). The 5 edge case tests cover headings-only, special characters, HTML tags, very long articles, and Unicode content. The 3 publish-then-validate integration tests verify cmd_publish output passes frontmatter validation for nextjs/hugo/astro. Test coverage grew from 134 to 142 (+8). The scoring benchmark partially addresses the 'scoring model uses regex heuristics' gap by providing known-input validation.

experts.rationale: Breadth: Scoring benchmarks, edge cases, and publish-then-validate integration tests are three new test categories. Depth: The benchmark function validates scoring model behavior against known inputs. The edge cases test real-world article variations. The publish-then-validate tests verify SSG frontmatter compatibility. Thickness: 142 tests. Scoring benchmarks, edge cases, and integration tests add meaningful coverage. Effectiveness: Scoring benchmarks validate the scoring model. Edge cases catch corner cases. Publish-then-validate catches SSG incompatibilities.

Breadth: Three new test categories expand coverage. Depth: The benchmark function validates scoring against known inputs. The edge cases cover Unicode, HTML, special characters. Thickness: 142 tests with good edge case coverage. Effectiveness: Benchmarks validate scoring model. Edge cases prevent regressions.

Breadth: Scoring benchmarks, edge cases, and publish-then-validate are all meaningful new categories. Depth: The benchmark function validates scoring model behavior, which directly addresses the regex heuristics gap. The edge cases test Unicode, HTML, special chars, and long articles. Thickness: 142 tests. The scoring benchmarks are particularly valuable — they validate the scoring model against expected behavior. Effectiveness: The benchmarks provide a regression baseline for the scoring model. The edge cases prevent corner-case bugs.

Breadth: Three new test categories. Depth: Scoring benchmarks validate model behavior. Edge cases cover real-world variations. Thickness: 142 tests with good diversity. Effectiveness: Benchmarks validate scoring model. Edge cases prevent regressions.

Breadth: Benchmarks and edge cases are real additions. But benchmarks validate that the model is internally consistent, not that it correlates with real search performance. Depth: The benchmarks test scoring against expected ranges, which is good internal validation. Edge cases cover real variations. Thickness: 142 tests. Good number. Effectiveness: Internal consistency is necessary but not sufficient for scoring model validation.

challenges.challenge: Lily scores breadth at 24, but scoring benchmarks validate internal consistency, not external validity. The benchmarks check that empty articles score low and dense articles score moderate, but they don't validate that scores correlate with search performance. I propose reducing from 24 to 23.

debate_rounds.title: Advocate Supports

supports.support: 142 tests including scoring benchmarks (3), edge cases (5), publish-then-validate integration (3), CLI smoke tests (3), multi-language tests (2), frontmatter validation (7), internal link suggestion (4), language phrases (2), scoring calibration (5), and 108+ other tests. The test coverage is comprehensive across unit, integration, and edge-case levels.

debate_rounds.statement: 142 tests across 18 test classes is strong coverage.

debate_rounds.title: Chair Synthesis
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 9 advances from 93 to 95. The scoring benchmarks and edge cases strengthen the quality assurance layer. The publish-then-validate integration tests partially address the SSG rendering gap. The project is at 95/100 — approaching the convergence threshold. The remaining 5 points represent genuinely hard gaps: (1) scoring model not validated against real search data (requires data collection), (2) draft generates scaffolding not content (requires LLM), (3) no actual SSG build test (requires SSG installation). These gaps require external dependencies that cannot be resolved within the current codebase.

gaps: Scoring model not validated against real search performance data (requires data collection)
Draft generates scaffolding, not full article content (requires LLM integration)
No actual SSG build test (requires SSG installation in CI)
No semantic relevance scoring for internal link suggestions
No automated A/B testing or performance tracking for published articles
